frameworks.name: Platform Economics Assessment
characteristics: Multi-sided: Connects different user groups (buyers/sellers, creators/consumers)
Network effects: Value increases with more participants
Data flywheel: Usage generates data that improves the product
Zero marginal cost: Adding users costs nearly nothing

frameworks.name: Winner-Take-All Assessment
frameworks.description: Determine if market dynamics favor monopoly outcomes

strong_winner_take_all: Standard setting (de facto or de jure)

frameworks.name: Disruption Assessment Framework
frameworks.description: Christensen's framework for identifying disruptive threats and opportunities

types.low_end: Starts with overserved customers who don't need full features
types.new_market: Creates new consumption where none existed before

assessment: 1. Is incumbent's product 'good enough' for most customers?
2. Is there a simpler/cheaper alternative emerging?
4. Is new entrant improving faster than customer needs evolve?

frameworks.name: Unit Economics Framework
frameworks.description: Evaluate the fundamental economics of each customer

ltv_cac.formula: LTV/CAC = (ARPU x Gross Margin x Lifetime) / CAC
ltv_cac.interpretation: Below 1x = losing money on each customer

cac_payback.interpretation: How long to recover customer acquisition cost

frameworks.name: S-Curve Adoption Analysis
frameworks.description: Determine where technology is on adoption curve

frameworks.name: Product-Market Fit Assessment
frameworks.description: Evaluate if product has achieved true product-market fit

strong_pmf: Users would be 'very disappointed' if product disappeared (>40%)
Usage increasing over time per cohort
